title: Tangy Saudi-Arabian Shoyu-Koji Shawarma
description: A tangy and crisp twist on the classic shawarma, featuring shoyu-koji and shio-cure. Perfect for adventurous foodies looking for a low-sodium option.

ingredients.name: Shoyu-Koji
ingredients.notes: available at most health food stores or online

ingredients.name: Mixed Greens
ingredients.notes: any combination of lettuce, cucumber, tomato, etc.

instructions: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
In a small bowl, whisk together shoyu-koji, salt, and black pepper.
Add the shio-cure lamb to the bowl and toss to coat with the shoyu-koji mixture.
Thread the lamb onto metal skewers, leaving a small amount of space between each piece.
Brush the pita bread with olive oil and grill for 1-2 minutes, until lightly toasted.
Add the lamb skewers to the grill and cook for 5-7 minutes, until cooked through.
Meanwhile, prepare the tzatziki sauce and pickled cucumber.
Once the lamb is cooked, remove from the grill and let rest for 1-2 minutes.
Warm the pita bread by wrapping it in foil and heating in the oven for 5 minutes.
Assemble the shawarma by placing a few pieces of lamb onto each pita bread, followed by a spoonful of tzatziki sauce, pickled cucumber, mixed greens, and red onion.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and serve immediately.
